task_completion.visual_evidence: The main Rocket.Chat workspace interface is visible, indicating the agent has successfully logged in.

task_completion.description: Verify navigation to the Administration panel.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Rocket.Chat Administration or Workspace settings menu is open on the screen.

task_completion.description: Verify navigation to the Custom User Status settings.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Custom User Status' configuration page or list is visible within the administration area.

task_completion.description: Verify the status name is set to 'Deploying'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The word 'Deploying' is visible in the Name input field for a new status, or listed as the name in the saved custom statuses list.

task_completion.description: Verify the presence type is set to 'Busy'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: 'Busy' is selected in the Presence Type dropdown menu, or shown as the presence type for the 'Deploying' status in the saved list.

task_completion.description: Verify the status text is set to 'Executing critical release deployment'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The exact text 'Executing critical release deployment' is visible in the Status Text input field, or displayed alongside the 'Deploying' status in the saved custom statuses list.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used a web browser to interact with Rocket.Chat.
integrity.visual_evidence: A web browser window is visible displaying the Rocket.Chat interface, rather than terminal API commands (like curl).

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the actual Rocket.Chat application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show standard Rocket.Chat UI elements (menus, sidebars, form fields) rather than a text editor or a static mock page.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the UI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Browser developer tools (Inspect Element console, DOM editor) are not open and actively modifying the page text to fake completion.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected custom user status name is 'Deploying'. The expected presence type is 'busy' (or 'Busy'). The expected status text/message is 'Executing critical release deployment'.
